tag_descriptions.Device Status:
Query the scanner's current state without changing any settings. These three read-only commands cover health monitoring, device identification, and regulatory configuration.

| Command | What It Returns |
|---|---|
| `get_status` | Real-time health — battery level, temperature, radio activity, power source, NTP clock sync, and terminal connection |
| `get_version` | Device identity — model, serial number, SKU, and all firmware versions (main, scanner, radio, IoTC) |
| `get_current_region` | Radio regulation — country code, frequency channels, power limits, frequency hopping, and LBT settings |

Use these commands for monitoring dashboards, firmware audits, and compliance checks.
tag_descriptions.Device Configuration:
Read or write the scanner's complete configuration in a single command.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `get_config` | Retrieve everything — equivalent to calling get_version + get_status + get_current_region + get_wifi + get_eth + get_endpoint_config + get_installed_certificate at once |
| `set_config` | Apply configuration changes — Wi-Fi, endpoint, events, and more in one payload |

`get_config` is read-only. `set_config` is the primary way to push settings to one or many scanners.
tag_descriptions.Network Configuration:
Manage the scanner's Wi-Fi and Ethernet network connections.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `get_wifi` | Current Wi-Fi status — connected SSID, IP address, signal, security type |
| `set_wifi` | Configure Wi-Fi — add/update SSID, set security (WPA2/WPA3/Enterprise), IP settings |
| `delete_wifi_profile` | Remove a saved Wi-Fi profile by SSID |
| `get_eth` | Ethernet status — link state, speed, IP address, 802.1X security |

The scanner supports both Wi-Fi and Ethernet simultaneously. Wi-Fi profiles are stored on device and auto-connect on reboot.
tag_descriptions.MQTT Endpoint Configuration:
Configure where the scanner sends data — the MQTT broker connection.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `config_endpoint` | Set up or update the MQTT broker — URL, port, protocol, credentials, topics, TLS settings |
| `get_endpoint_config` | Retrieve the current MQTT endpoint configuration |

The scanner communicates with your backend via MQTT. These commands control the broker URL, publish/subscribe topics, QoS levels, keep-alive, and reconnect behavior.
tag_descriptions.Certificate Management:
Manage TLS/SSL certificates for secure MQTT and Wi-Fi Enterprise connections.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `install_certificate` | Upload a certificate (CA, client, MQTT, Wi-Fi) to the scanner |
| `delete_certificate` | Remove an installed certificate by name |
| `get_installed_certificate` | List all certificates currently installed on the device |

Certificates are required for MQTT-TLS connections and WPA2/WPA3 Enterprise Wi-Fi authentication.
tag_descriptions.System Operations:
Perform system-level actions on the scanner.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `reboot` | Restart the scanner — applies pending configuration changes |
| `set_os` | Trigger a firmware update on the device |

⚠️ Both commands interrupt normal scanner operation. `reboot` requires no active inventory. `set_os` requires sufficient battery level.
tag_descriptions.Inventory Control:
Start and stop RFID tag reading (inventory) operations.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `control_operation` | Start or stop an RFID inventory scan |

When started, the scanner continuously reads RFID tags and publishes tag data events to the MQTT broker. Use `stop` to end the scan.
tag_descriptions.Operating Mode:
Configure how the scanner reads tags — mode, power, and session settings.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `set_operating_mode` | Set the RFID operating mode — transmit power, session, tag population, and more |
| `get_operating_mode` | Retrieve the current RFID operating mode settings |

Operating mode controls the RF parameters that affect read range, speed, and tag population handling.
tag_descriptions.Tag Filtering:
Filter which tags are reported after an inventory scan.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `set_post_filter` | Define filter rules — match by EPC pattern, memory bank, or mask to include/exclude specific tags |
| `get_post_filter` | Retrieve the current tag filter configuration |

Post-filters are applied after the radio reads tags, reducing noise by only reporting tags that match your criteria.
tag_descriptions.Device Health:
Periodic heartbeat events sent by the scanner to confirm it is alive and connected.

| Event | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `heartBeatEVT` | Periodic health pulse — battery status, inventory state, and connectivity confirmation |

Heartbeat interval is configurable via `set_config` → `eventConfiguration` → `heartbeatConfiguration`. If heartbeats stop arriving, the scanner may be offline or disconnected.
tag_descriptions.Alerts:
Alert events triggered by significant device state changes.

| Event | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `alerts` | Full alert details — battery, temperature, power, network, firmware, and antenna alerts |
| `alert_short` | Compact alert summary for lightweight monitoring |

Alerts are push-based — the scanner publishes them automatically when a threshold is crossed or a state changes. Enable/disable specific alerts via `set_config` → `eventConfiguration`.
tag_descriptions.Exceptions:
Exception events for errors and unexpected conditions.

| Event | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `exceptionEVT` | Error reports — radio failures, configuration errors, and other exceptional conditions |

Exceptions indicate something went wrong that may require operator attention or automated recovery.
tag_descriptions.MQTT Connectivity:
MQTT connection state change events.

| Event | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `mqttConnEVT` | Broker connection/disconnection events — tracks when the scanner connects to or loses connection with the MQTT broker |

Use these events to monitor broker connectivity across your fleet and trigger reconnection alerts.
tag_descriptions.Event Configuration:
Configure which events the scanner publishes to the MQTT broker.

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `config_events` | Enable or disable specific events and alerts — heartbeat, battery, temperature, network, firmware, NTP, etc. |

Event configuration controls which device events are forwarded to the broker. You can also set the heartbeat interval and choose what data to include in heartbeats.
tag_descriptions.Tag Data Event:
RFID tag read data published during an active inventory scan.

| Event | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `dataEVT` | Tag data — EPC, RSSI, antenna, timestamp, and optional memory bank data for each tag read |

Tag data events stream continuously while inventory is running. Each event contains one or more tag reads with EPC (Electronic Product Code), signal strength, and read metadata.
